entity_channel_mapping.description: Select the entities and actions that provide and control each output channel ({channels}). The entity you selected is pre-filled as the read source.

device_info.title: Add Physical Device
device_info.description:
To create your device, consider splitting it into several logical vdSDs:
- Are parts physically separable? → use individual vdSDs.
- Does the (sub-)device contain several sensors? → keep as one vdSD.
- Does the (sub-)device contain different kinds of capabilities (Button, Binary Input, Sensors, Output)? → keep as one vdSD.
- Do functional units belong to different dS classes (colors)? → separate vdSDs.
- Can functional units end up in different zones? → separate vdSDs.
- Does the device contain several different outputs (not channels!)? → one vdSD per output required.
- Are there multiple buttons/binary inputs that shall be configurable via DSS configurator? → separate vdSDs (max. one (Multi)button and one binary input per vdSD).

data.vendorName: Vendor name of your device (e.g. Occhio)

data_description.vendorName: Manufacturer or vendor of the physical hardware device (free-form string).
data_description.displayId: Device model/type name as printed on the physical device or used for identification (e.g. 'Lunanova').

data.modelVersion: Exact model name of this component (e.g. Lunanova PowerUnit)
data.identify_action: HA action to identify the device (optional)
data.firmwareUpdate_action: HA action to trigger firmware update of device / component (optional)

data_description.displayId: Name or label for this virtualDC sub-device component as it will appear in the dSS configurator.
data_description.primaryGroup: Main dS color group / device class for this vdSD: 1=lights, 2=blinds, 3=climate/heating.
data_description.modelVersion: Exact model variant string, used to distinguish different configurations or firmware variants.
data_description.identify_action: Home Assistant action to call when dSS requests visual identification of this device (e.g. blink an indicator light).
data_description.firmwareUpdate_action: Home Assistant action to call when dSS requests a firmware update for this device.

data.name: Name of this button / element
data.channel: Output channel controlled by this button
data.supportsLocalKeyMode: Button can act as a local button
data.callsPresent: Button press sets absence state to present
data.callbackType: How should button presses be forwarded to dS?

data_description.name: Human-readable label for this button element, matching the physical hardware connector or panel label.
data_description.buttonType: Physical button type: 0=undefined, 1=single pushbutton, 2=two-way rocker, 3=4-way navigation, 4=4-way with center, 5=8-way with center, 6=on/off switch.
data_description.group: dS color group this button sends commands to, e.g. 1=lights, 2=blinds, 3=climate.
data_description.function: Scope of the button's action: 5=room, higher values expand to zone or apartment level.
data_description.mode: Button pairing and behavior mode: 0=standard, 5–8=button 1–4 down (paired), 9–12=button 1–4 up, 255=inactive.
data_description.channel: Which output channel this button controls: 0=default channel, 1–191=standard dS channel types.
data_description.supportsLocalKeyMode: If enabled, this button can operate locally on the device without requiring a dSS connection.
data_description.setsLocalPriority: If enabled, pressing this button sets local priority on the output, causing it to ignore scene calls until priority is cleared.
data_description.callsPresent: If enabled, pressing this button switches the system presence state from absent to present.
data_description.callbackType: How button press events are delivered to dSS: 'clickTypes' passes the raw click type as entity state, 'detect_clicks' auto-detects event type from binary sensor, event, or button entities.

data.name: Name of the binary input
data.group: dS group the binary input belongs to
data.sensorFunction: Function controlled by this binary input
data.hardwiredFunction: Is the function hardwired or configurable at runtime?
data.updateInterval: Time (in seconds) the hardware updates the value
data.inputType: Is the value polled by DSS or pushed at change?
data.inputUsage: Usage field of the binary input
data.valueType: Does the binary input deliver boolean or integer values?
data.callback_entity: Entity providing binary input state value

data_description.name: Human-readable label for this binary input, matching the physical hardware connector or panel label.
data_description.group: dS color group this binary input belongs to.
data_description.sensorFunction: What this binary input detects: e.g. 1=presence, 5=motion, 7=smoke, 13=window contact, 15=window handle.
data_description.hardwiredFunction: If non-zero, the sensor function is fixed by hardware and cannot be changed at runtime. Value 0 means freely configurable.
data_description.updateInterval: How frequently the hardware tracks and reports the physical value, in seconds.
data_description.inputType: How the dSS receives the value: 0=polled by dSS on request, 1=the hardware pushes updates when the value changes.
data_description.inputUsage: Usage context of this input: 0=undefined, 1=room climate, 2=outdoor climate, 3=climate setting.

data.name: Name of the sensor
data.resolution: Sensor resolution — smallest deviation that can be displayed (LSB)
data.updateInterval: Time (in seconds) the hardware tracks the sensor value
data.aliveSignInterval: Maximum time (in seconds) between updates before sensor is considered out of order
data.minPushInterval: Minimum time (in seconds) to wait between pushes of changed sensor values (default: 2 s)
data.changesOnlyInterval: Minimum time (in seconds) between pushes of unchanged values (0 = push all updates)

data_description.name: Human-readable label for this sensor, matching the physical hardware connector or panel label.
data_description.group: dS color group this sensor belongs to.
data_description.sensorType: Physical quantity this sensor measures: e.g. 1=temperature (°C), 2=humidity (%), 3=illuminance (lux), 5=CO (ppm), 13=wind speed (m/s), 14=active power (W).
data_description.sensorUsage: Usage context: 0=undefined, 1=room, 2=outdoor, 3=user interaction/dial, 4=device total, 5=device last-run, 6=device average.
data_description.min: Minimum physical value the sensor hardware can deliver (in the unit defined by sensorType).
data_description.max: Maximum physical value the sensor hardware can deliver (in the unit defined by sensorType).
data_description.resolution: Smallest value change the sensor hardware can detect — the size of one LSB of the hardware sensor.
data_description.updateInterval: Approximate time between hardware value updates in seconds.
data_description.aliveSignInterval: Maximum time in seconds between sensor updates before the sensor is considered out-of-order.
data_description.minPushInterval: Minimum time in seconds between consecutive push reports to dSS (throttle). Default is 2 seconds.
data_description.changesOnlyInterval: Minimum time in seconds between pushes of the same (unchanged) value. Default 0 means every update triggers a push.

data_description.name: Human-readable label for this output, matching the physical hardware connector or panel label.
data_description.groups: Set of dS color groups this output can operate in, e.g. 1=lights, 2=blinds, 3=climate. An output may belong to multiple groups.
data_description.defaultGroup: Primary dS color group for this output, used when no explicit group is specified.
data_description.function: Output capability: 0=on/off only, 1=dimmer, 2=positional (blinds/valves), 3=dimmer+color temp, 4=full color (RGB+CT), 5=bipolar (heating/cooling valve).
data_description.outputUsage: Usage context of this output: 0=undefined, 1=room, 2=outdoors, 3=user display/indicator.
data_description.variableRamp: If enabled, this output supports configurable ramp/transition speeds (dimTimeUp/dimTimeDown settings become meaningful).

data.onThreshold: Minimum output value that switches on the output (default: 50)
data.minBrightness: Minimum output value the hardware supports (e.g. dimming value at which a bulb ignites)
data.maxPower: Maximum power the output can deliver (W)
data.activeCoolingMode: Output provides active cooling capabilities (e.g. air-conditioning, FCU)

data_description.onThreshold: For on/off and dimmable outputs, the brightness level (0–100%) at which the output switches ON. Defaults to 50%.
data_description.minBrightness: Minimum brightness level (0–100%) the hardware supports, e.g. the ignition threshold for certain bulbs.
data_description.maxPower: Maximum power output in watts. If not set, the power capability is undefined.
data_description.activeCoolingMode: If enabled, this output can actively cool (e.g. air conditioning unit or fan coil unit).

data.channelType: Type of channel to add to the output
data.name: Name of this channel
data.resolution: Channel resolution — smallest deviation that can be displayed (LSB)

data_description.channelType: Numerical type ID of this control channel as defined by digitalSTROM: e.g. brightness, hue, saturation, color temperature, position, angle.
data_description.name: Human-readable label for this output channel, matching the physical hardware connector or panel label.
data_description.min: Minimum physical value this channel can accept or deliver.
data_description.max: Maximum physical value this channel can accept or deliver.
data_description.resolution: Smallest step (LSB) this channel can resolve — the finest granularity of control available.

channel_mapping.title: Bind Output Channels to HA
channel_mapping.description: Select the entities that reflect the channel values of the output.

vdsd_name.description: This vdSD has multiple artefacts. Enter a name for the Home Assistant device and the digitalSTROM virtual device.
